prerequisites: An AWS account with access to Amazon S3.

An existing S3 bucket. You provide its name in the bucket config field. Buckets are created in a specific AWS Region that cannot be changed after creation, so note the bucket Region for the region_name field.
IAM permissions to read the target data: s3:ListBucket on the bucket and s3:GetObject on its objects. Grant these via an IAM policy attached to the user or role.
The path_pattern, format, and streams settings that describe which files to read and how to parse them. streams is required.
For non-AWS, S3-compatible storage: the service endpoint URL (endpoint config field) and provider settings.
For cross-account or role-based access: an IAM role you are permitted to assume, identified by its role ARN (role_arn config field).

authMethods.name: AWS access keys (IAM user credentials)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with an access key ID and secret access key, supplied in the aws_access_key_id and aws_secret_access_key fields (also available under provider.aws_access_key_id / provider.aws_secret_access_key). These are long-term credentials for an IAM user. An access key has two parts that must be used together, the secret access key is shown only once at creation time, and a user can have a maximum of two access keys. The associated identity needs s3:ListBucket and s3:GetObject permission on the target bucket.

authMethods.name: IAM role assumption (role ARN)
authMethods.summary: Instead of, or in addition to, static keys, supply an IAM role ARN in the role_arn field to access the bucket by assuming that role. This is the recommended pattern for cross-account access, where a role in the bucket-owning account is assumed to read its objects.

setupSteps.title: Identify the bucket and its Region
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the AWS Management Console and open the Amazon S3 console. Locate the bucket holding your data and note its name and AWS Region. Set bucket to the bucket name and region_name to its Region. A bucket's Region is fixed at creation and cannot be changed.

setupSteps.title: Grant read permissions
setupSteps.body: Ensure the IAM user or role used for the connection has read access to the data: s3:ListBucket on the bucket (arn:aws:s3:::your-bucket) and s3:GetObject on its objects (arn:aws:s3:::your-bucket/*). Attach these as an IAM policy. Scope the resource to a prefix if you only need part of the bucket.

setupSteps.title: Create access keys (key-based auth)
setupSteps.body: In the IAM console, create an access key for the IAM user. Copy both the access key ID and the secret access key immediately, because the secret is shown only at creation time. Put them in aws_access_key_id and aws_secret_access_key. A user can hold at most two access keys.

setupSteps.title: Or configure a role ARN (role-based auth)
setupSteps.body: For cross-account or role-based access, set role_arn to the ARN of the IAM role to assume. The role's trust policy must permit your principal to assume it, and the role must hold the s3:ListBucket and s3:GetObject permissions on the bucket.

setupSteps.title: Configure the files to read
setupSteps.body: Set path_pattern to match the object keys (prefixes/paths) you want, choose the file format in format, and define the streams to extract. Optionally set start_date to limit by modification time, dataset to name the data, and schema if you supply an explicit schema.

setupSteps.title: Configure S3-compatible storage (optional)
setupSteps.body: To read from non-AWS, S3-compatible object storage, set endpoint to the service endpoint URL and configure the provider settings accordingly. Leave these unset for standard Amazon S3.
